---
title: "ShinyApps Web Traffic Monitoring"
format: 
  dashboard:
    theme: litera
    orientation: rows
---

<!-- 
HOW TO RUN THIS DASHBOARD FOR CODE REVIEW:
Option 1: In Positron or RStudio, open this file and click the "Render" button at the top.
Option 2: Run the following in the R Console: `quarto::quarto_render("Report/dashboard.qmd")`
Option 3: Run the following in the Terminal: `quarto preview Report/dashboard.qmd`
-->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(knitr)
library(ggplot2)
library(forcats)
library(plotly)
library(safepaths)
library(glue)
library(DT)

# Define paths based on your 00-setup.R logic
# and the working directory during render is the R/ folder.
LAN_FOLDER <- use_network_path()
PROJECT_ROOT <- glue("{LAN_FOLDER}/0. Misc/Data Science Tooling/web-hosting-and-dashboards/shinyapps_webtraffic_monitoring")
OUTPUT_TABLES <- glue("{PROJECT_ROOT}/outputs/tables")

# Load summary data for value boxes
weekly_usage <- read_csv(file.path(OUTPUT_TABLES, "weekly_usage.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
visits_summary <- read_csv(file.path(OUTPUT_TABLES, "visits_minmax_summary.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
concurrency_summary <- read_csv(file.path(OUTPUT_TABLES, "concurrency_summary.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE)

latest_week <- max(weekly_usage$week, na.rm = TRUE)
latest_week_data <- weekly_usage |> filter(week == latest_week)

top_app <- latest_week_data |> arrange(desc(total_sessions)) |> slice(1) |> pull(pageTitle)
latest_visits <- sum(latest_week_data$total_sessions, na.rm = TRUE)
peak_concurrent <- max(concurrency_summary$max_concurrent, na.rm = TRUE)
```

# Executive Summary

## Row {height=35%}

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Most Popular App (Latest Week)"
list(
  icon = "star",
  color = "primary",
  value = top_app
)
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Total Visits (Latest Week, All Apps)"
list(
  icon = "graph-up",
  color = "info",
  value = latest_visits
)
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Max Concurrent Users"
list(
  icon = "people-fill",
  color = "success",
  value = peak_concurrent
)
```

## Row {height=65%}

### Weekly App Visits Trend

```{r}
visits_trend_plot <- weekly_usage |>
  ggplot(aes(x = week, y = total_sessions, color = pageTitle)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.5) +
  labs(
    title = "Weekly App Visits Trend",
    x = "Week",
    y = "Total Visits (Sessions)",
    color = "App"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 22, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 18),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 18),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 14)
  )

ggplotly(visits_trend_plot)
```

### Maximum Concurrent Visits

```{r}
max_concurrent_plot <- concurrency_summary |>
  filter(interval == "1m") |> # Use 1-minute buckets for true peak
  mutate(app_name = fct_reorder(app_name, max_concurrent, .desc = TRUE)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = app_name, y = max_concurrent, fill = app_name)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = max_concurrent), vjust = -0.5, size = 5) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1))) + # Add space for labels
  labs(
    title = "Maximum Concurrent Visits by App",
    subtitle = "Based on 1-minute interval peaks",
    x = "App",
    y = "Max Concurrent Users"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 22, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 18),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 14),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14)
  )

ggplotly(max_concurrent_plot, tooltip = c("x", "y"))
```


# App Details {scrolling="true"}

## Row {.tabset height=800px}

### Traffic Distribution (High Traffic)

```{r}
high_traffic_apps <- c("popApp", "Student Outcomes")

visits_minmax_plot_high <- weekly_usage |>
  filter(pageTitle %in% high_traffic_apps) |>
  mutate(
    pageTitle = fct_reorder(pageTitle, total_sessions, .fun = max, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = pageTitle, y = total_sessions, fill = pageTitle)) +
  geom_boxplot(
    alpha = 0.7,
    outlier.size = 3,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Weekly Visits (High Traffic Apps)",
    subtitle = "Box shows 25th to 75th percentile, line is median, dots are outliers",
    x = "App",
    y = "Weekly Visits"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 22, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 18),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 14),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14)
  )

ggplotly(visits_minmax_plot_high)
```

### Traffic Distribution (Low Traffic)

```{r}
visits_minmax_plot_low <- weekly_usage |>
  filter(!pageTitle %in% high_traffic_apps) |>
  mutate(
    pageTitle = fct_reorder(pageTitle, total_sessions, .fun = max, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = pageTitle, y = total_sessions, fill = pageTitle)) +
  geom_boxplot(
    alpha = 0.7,
    outlier.size = 3,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(
    ylim = c(
      0,
      quantile(
        weekly_usage |>
          filter(!pageTitle %in% high_traffic_apps) |>
          pull(total_sessions),
        0.95,
        na.rm = TRUE
      )
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Weekly Visits (Low Traffic Apps)",
    subtitle = "Box shows 25th to 75th percentile, line is median, dots are outliers (Y-axis zoomed)",
    x = "App",
    y = "Weekly Visits"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 22, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 18),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 14),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14)
  )

ggplotly(visits_minmax_plot_low)
```

### Trend by App

```{r}
visits_trend_faceted_plot <- weekly_usage |>
  ggplot(aes(x = week, y = total_sessions, color = pageTitle)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.5, show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~pageTitle, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Weekly App Visits Trend (By App)",
    x = "Week",
    y = "Total Visits"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 22, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 18),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 12),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold")
  )

ggplotly(visits_trend_faceted_plot)
```

## Row

### Summary Statistics Table

```{r}
visits_summary_clean <- visits_summary |>
  rename(
    `App Name` = pageTitle,
    `Min Weekly Visits` = min_weekly_visits,
    `Median Weekly Visits` = median_weekly_visits,
    `Avg Weekly Visits` = avg_weekly_visits,
    `Max Weekly Visits` = max_weekly_visits
  ) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), round, 1))

datatable(visits_summary_clean, options = list(pageLength = 11, dom = 't'))
```
